headlineSummary: HN sentiment skews negative for Anthropic on trust, pricing, and benchmark concerns, while OpenAI reads mixed-to-neutral with both criticism and praise [E1][E3][E4][E6].

ranking.summary: Anthropic draws mostly negative sentiment, with commenters questioning claim credibility, fairness of pricing and service, benchmark integrity, and possible disappointing model quality, though one user reports effective use of Opus 4.6 [E2][E3][E4][E5][E6].
OpenAI is mixed to neutral: one comment calls its numbers "garbage," but others say GPT 5.4 and Codex 5.4 performed well in real use [E1][E2][E7].

evidence.excerpt: I'm not defending anthropic and openai either. Their numbers are garbage too since they don't produce false-positive rates either.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says Anthropic's reported numbers are 'garbage,' criticizing the credibility of its claims.
The same sentence explicitly includes OpenAI and says its numbers are 'garbage,' a direct negative judgment about reported results.

evidence.excerpt: I know this same technique is effective with Opus 4.6 and GPT 5.4 because I've been using it on my own code.

annotations.rationale: Opus 4.6 is presented as effective in the user's own coding experience, which is mild praise for Anthropic model capability.
GPT 5.4 is explicitly described as effective on the user's own code, a positive assessment of OpenAI model capability.

evidence.excerpt: The people at Anthropic make a bunch of claims and the public is expected to just believe them without any possibility of testing those claims or reproducing those results

annotations.rationale: This strongly accuses Anthropic of making unverifiable claims and expecting blind trust, which is severe criticism of credibility.

evidence.excerpt: subscription plan users are getting a much degraded service for the same money through both open and hidden policies, while Anthropic moves compute to serve off-the-counter customers.

annotations.rationale: The commenter alleges degraded service at the same price and unfavorable allocation of compute, a strong negative claim about Anthropic's customer treatment.

evidence.excerpt: part of the reason Anthropic hasn't immediately released Mythos is that it would be slightly disappointing relative to the benchmark scores.

annotations.rationale: The commenter suggests Anthropic is delaying release because the model would disappoint versus benchmarks, implying underwhelming quality.

evidence.excerpt: Anthropic's 4.6 series is the first frontier model to have serious training set contamination on Splunk BOTS.

annotations.rationale: Calling Anthropic's 4.6 series the first frontier model with 'serious training set contamination' is a direct negative claim about benchmark integrity.

evidence.excerpt: I gave Codex 5.4 Playwright MCP access to the site and a prompt of "Use Playwright CLI Skill to open https://playstarfling.com/ and load the game. Work out how to play it, and devise a strategy to win." After a about half a dozen attempts it had figured the game out.

annotations.rationale: Codex 5.4 is described as learning how to play and win after several attempts, which is a positive report of OpenAI model capability.
